# Plan-Issue Commands — Design Spec

**Date:** 2026-05-08
**Status:** Draft
**Scope:** Three Claude Code slash commands that turn an open spec PR (generated by `/spec-issue` or `/spec-issues`) into a spec+plan PR — adding an implementation plan inline to the existing spec branch. Mirrors the spec-issue commands in shape and discipline.

## Goal

Same lifecycle pattern as `/spec-issue` → `/spec-review`, extended with `/plan-issue` → `/plan-review`. After a spec PR has been generated and reviewed, the author runs `/plan-issue <N>` to add a step-by-step implementation plan as a new commit on the same spec branch. The PR title is updated to `Spec + Plan: <title> (#N)`. The author then runs `/plan-review <N>` to walk the plan via the brainstorming skill and apply revisions.

## Why

Spec PRs are the design artefact. Implementation plans are the execution artefact. Today both already live in the repo (`docs/superpowers/specs/`, `docs/superpowers/plans/`), but only the spec half is generated by command. The plan half is still hand-written, which is fine for one-off work but tedious across a backlog of issues that already have spec PRs ready to plan.

The same justification as the spec-issue commands applies: keep generation local (no GitHub Action, no `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` repo secret), use Claude Code's native tools, match the proven shape of `/spec-issue`.

## Non-goals

- **No GitHub Action.** Local execution only.
- **No new directory structure.** Plans live under `docs/superpowers/plans/` exactly as today.
- **No discovery report for plans.** The plan file is the artefact. The spec PR's body already carries the spec's discovery report from `/spec-issue`.
- **No separate plan branch / plan PR.** The plan is a new commit on the existing spec branch; the existing draft PR is updated in place. (Considered and rejected: stacked plan branch with its own PR — adds review overhead without benefit, and decouples spec/plan in a way that invites drift.)
- **No automatic spec→plan handoff.** `/plan-issue` is run by the author when they're ready, after `/spec-review` is done.
- **No support for plans on closed/merged spec PRs.** If the spec PR is closed, refuse — the author can manually create a follow-up branch off main.
- **No parallel sweep.** Sequential per issue (same reason as spec sweep: `git checkout` on a shared working tree).

## Decisions

### Surface

Three commands in `.claude/commands/`:

- **`/plan-issue <issue-number>`** (new) — adds an implementation plan as a commit on the open spec branch for issue N, updates the existing PR title and body.
- **`/plan-issues`** (new) — sweep: for every issue with an open spec PR but no plan file on the branch, dispatch a subagent to run `/plan-issue N`. Sequential.
- **`/plan-review <issue-number>`** (new) — locate the spec+plan PR for issue N, check it out, load context, walk the plan via the `superpowers:brainstorming` skill, apply revisions to the plan file.

### Output shape

Per processed issue:

- New commit on `spec/issue-N-<slug>` (the existing spec branch) adding `docs/superpowers/plans/<run-date>-issue-N-<slug>.md`.
- Existing PR title updated from `Spec: <title> (#N)` to `Spec + Plan: <title> (#N)`.
- Existing PR body has a new trailing section appended:
  ```markdown
  ---

  ## Plan

  Plan file: `docs/superpowers/plans/<run-date>-issue-N-<slug>.md`

  ### Tasks
  - Task 1: <one-line summary>
  - Task 2: <one-line summary>
  - ...

  *Generated by /plan-issue. Review before merge.*
  ```
  The original `## Discovery & Decisions` block from the spec PR is preserved in full.
- Comment on issue #N: `Plan added to spec PR: <url>` (warn-only on failure — same discipline as spec-issue).

### Pre-flight checks (`/plan-issue`)

Refuses to start when:

- `gh issue view <N> --json state` returns anything other than `OPEN`.
- The issue does not have an open spec PR. The check: `gh pr list --state open --search "head:spec/issue-N-" --json number,headRefName,title,url`. Must find exactly one match — zero or many is an error.
- Working tree has uncommitted changes (`git status --porcelain` non-empty).
- After checking out the spec branch (with `gh pr checkout <pr-number>`) and pulling, a file matching `docs/superpowers/plans/*-issue-N-*.md` already exists.

All four refusals print clear, actionable error messages and return the user to the default branch.

### Context gathering

Free exploration via Claude Code's native tools. After checking out the spec branch:

- **Always read:** the spec file on this branch (`docs/superpowers/specs/*-issue-N-*-design.md`), `CLAUDE.md`, the issue body via `gh issue view --json title,body,labels`, and the 3 most recent files in `docs/superpowers/plans/*.md` (excluding any with a `Superseded` header in the first ~10 lines, ordered by mtime via `ls -t`).
- **Then explore as needed:** read app source files relevant to the implementation. The spec already names architectural decisions; the plan needs to know the *current* file shapes to write accurate "modify X line Y" steps. Examples: a spec that introduces a new screen → read existing similar screens for the ViewModel pattern; a spec that adds a DAO query → read the existing DAO and entity files.
- **Track the reads.** Maintain a list of issue-specific files read; cite them in the plan's "Reference" or "File Map" section as evidence the plan is grounded.

### Design step (writing the plan)

Match the format and tone of the prior plans (e.g., `2026-05-08-spec-issue-commands.md`). The plan file must contain, in roughly this order:

- Title heading: `# <Issue Title> — Implementation Plan`
- Required-skill banner: `> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (\`- [ ]\`) syntax for tracking.`
- **Goal:** one-paragraph synthesis of the spec.
- **Architecture:** one paragraph on layering / file-level structure.
- **Tech Stack:** the relevant subset (Kotlin, Compose, Room, etc., per `CLAUDE.md`).
- **Reference:** path to the spec file (relative), with a note about which decisions to read first.
- **File Map:** table of files to create/modify/delete with one-line per row.
- **Branch strategy:** which branch the plan applies to (the spec branch).
- **Tasks:** numbered tasks, each broken into `- [ ]` Step blocks. Each Step contains the exact commands or edits.
- **Phase summary:** small table at the end mapping phases to tasks.

Each task must be self-contained enough that an agentic worker can execute it without re-deriving context. Step bodies cite file paths and line ranges where possible. Match the verbosity and structure of the most recent prior plan.

### PR body update

The existing spec PR body is preserved in full. The new `## Plan` section is appended at the end (separated by `---`). Update is via `gh pr edit <pr-number> --body-file <tmpfile>` where the temp file contains the original body + the new section.

The PR title is updated via the same `gh pr edit` call: `--title "Spec + Plan: <title> (#N)"`.

### Slug + filenames

The slug rules are unchanged from `/spec-issue` — and `/plan-issue` doesn't compute the slug from the issue title. Instead it extracts the slug from the spec branch name: given the head branch `spec/issue-N-<slug>`, parse off the `spec/issue-N-` prefix. This guarantees the plan file's slug matches the spec file's slug exactly (no risk of drift if the issue title changed since `/spec-issue` ran).

- Plan file path: `docs/superpowers/plans/<today>-issue-N-<slug>.md` (no `-design` suffix — matches existing plan convention).
- Branch: unchanged. The plan is added to the existing `spec/issue-N-<slug>` branch.

### Cleanup discipline

`/plan-issue` always returns to the default branch on exit, success or failure. Same discipline as `/spec-issue`. The `gh pr checkout` step is what brings the user onto the spec branch; the final step is always `git checkout <default-branch>`.

### Sweep behaviour (`/plan-issues`)

1. **List open spec PRs.** `gh pr list --state open --search "head:spec/issue-" --json number,headRefName,title,url --limit 200`.
2. **Filter to issues whose tied spec branch lacks a plan file.** For each PR, extract the issue number from the head branch (`spec/issue-N-...`). Then enumerate the branch's `docs/superpowers/plans/` directory:
   - `git fetch origin --quiet`
   - `git ls-tree -r --name-only origin/spec/issue-N-<slug> -- docs/superpowers/plans/`
   - If any line matches `*-issue-N-*.md`, skip this issue.
3. **Display the eligible list and ask for confirmation** (same UX as `/spec-issues`). Print the issue number, title, and existing PR URL per row.
4. **Dispatch sequentially** via Task tool subagents, each running the body of `/plan-issue <N>`. Same rationale as spec sweep — the working tree is shared.
5. **Rollup** at the end: `processed=X failed=Y`, with PR URLs for successes and one-line reasons for failures. Sanity-check that the working tree is clean and on the default branch.

### Failure handling (consolidated)

| Scenario | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `gh` not authenticated / no network | Surface error, abort. |
| Issue not OPEN | Refuse: `issue #N is not open (state=closed)`. No checkout, no commit. |
| No open spec PR for the issue | Refuse: `no open spec PR found for issue #N — run /spec-issue N first`. |
| Multiple open spec PRs for the issue | Refuse: `found multiple open spec PRs for issue #N — resolve manually`. |
| Plan file already exists on spec branch | Refuse with the file path; suggest `/plan-review N` if the user wants to revise. |
| Working tree dirty | Refuse: `commit or stash before running /plan-issue`. |
| `git push` fails | Plan file committed locally; report the local branch and that push + PR-edit steps were skipped. |
| `gh pr edit` fails (after a successful push) | Plan is on origin but PR title/body not updated; report this state explicitly. |
| `gh issue comment` fails | Warn-only; PR is the canonical artefact. |
| Mid-sweep issue failure | Logged, sweep continues, rollup reports the breakdown. |
| User interrupts mid-sweep | Whatever's been processed stays; rollup shows partial results. |

### `/plan-review` body

Mirrors `/spec-review` very closely. Differences are minimal:

- Locate the PR by issue number (same query as `/spec-review` — `gh pr list --search "head:spec/issue-N-" --state open` is more reliable than the title-based search).
- After `gh pr checkout`, read into context: the **plan** file (`docs/superpowers/plans/*-issue-N-*.md`), the spec file, the PR body, the original issue body. (The plan-review skill uses the spec as context for the plan — same way the plan was generated.)
- Refuse if no plan file is found on the branch — `/plan-review` is for reviewing plans, not generating them; if the user wants to add a plan, they need `/plan-issue` first.
- Frame the brainstorming session around plan decisions: task ordering, completeness of step-by-step instructions, missing verification commands, risk areas, scope of each task, alignment with the spec.
- Apply user-approved revisions: edit the plan file in place, `git add <file> && git commit -m "plan: revise <area>"`, push.
- Do **not** mark the PR ready or merge.

## Architecture / affected components

```
.claude/
└── commands/
    ├── plan-issue.md                  (NEW — single-issue plan command)
    ├── plan-issues.md                 (NEW — sweep command)
    └── plan-review.md                 (NEW — review command)

docs/
└── superpowers/
    ├── specs/
    │   └── 2026-05-08-plan-issue-commands-design.md   (THIS FILE)
    └── plans/
        └── 2026-05-08-plan-issue-commands.md          (companion plan, written next)
```

No app source code touched. No existing commands modified. No deletions. The change set is purely additive.

## Slash command structure

Each command is a markdown file with YAML frontmatter (`description`, `argument-hint` where applicable) and a step-by-step body Claude follows when invoked. No bash helpers, no separate executables.

### `plan-issue.md` body outline

1. Validate `$ARGUMENTS` is a positive integer.
2. `gh issue view $ARGUMENTS --json state,title,body,labels` — must be `OPEN`.
3. `gh pr list --state open --search "head:spec/issue-$ARGUMENTS-" --json number,headRefName,title,body,url` — must find exactly one. Capture PR number, head branch, title, body, URL.
4. `git status --porcelain` — must be empty.
5. Resolve default branch.
6. `git fetch origin --quiet`.
7. `gh pr checkout <pr-number>` (this places HEAD on the spec branch and pulls).
8. Check `ls docs/superpowers/plans/ 2>/dev/null | grep -E "issue-$ARGUMENTS-" | head -1` — refuse if any match.
9. Extract slug from head branch name by stripping `spec/issue-N-` prefix.
10. Read priors: `CLAUDE.md`, the spec file on this branch (`docs/superpowers/specs/*-issue-N-*-design.md`), the 3 most recent prior plans (excluding superseded), the issue body.
11. Read issue-specific source files for grounding.
12. Today's date (UTC).
13. Plan file path: `docs/superpowers/plans/<today>-issue-N-<slug>.md`.
14. Design and write the plan file.
15. `git add <plan-file> && git commit -m "plan: draft for issue #N"`.
16. `git push origin <head-branch>`.
17. Build the appended `## Plan` section. Concatenate to the original PR body. Write to a temp file.
18. `gh pr edit <pr-number> --title "Spec + Plan: <title> (#N)" --body-file <tmpfile>`.
19. `gh issue comment N --body "Plan added to spec PR: <url>"` (warn-on-fail).
20. `git checkout <default-branch>` (always).
21. Report success: PR URL, plan file path, branch, suggest `/plan-review N`.

### `plan-issues.md` body outline

1. `gh pr list --state open --search "head:spec/issue-" --json number,headRefName,title,url --limit 200`.
2. For each PR, extract issue number from the head branch.
3. Check the eligible-issue's branch for an existing plan file: `git ls-tree -r --name-only origin/<head-branch> -- docs/superpowers/plans/ | grep -E "issue-N-.*\\.md"`. Skip if non-empty.
4. (Optional sanity) Confirm each candidate issue is still `OPEN` via `gh issue view`. Skip stale.
5. Display eligible list and ask for confirmation.
6. For each eligible issue, sequentially: dispatch a Task subagent with `/plan-issue <N>` instructions, wait for completion, capture result.
7. Rollup: `processed=X failed=Y` plus per-issue results.

### `plan-review.md` body outline

Mirrors the existing `/spec-review` structure:

1. Locate the spec+plan PR for issue N: `gh pr list --state open --search "head:spec/issue-$ARGUMENTS-" --json number,headRefName,title,url`. If multiple, prefer the most recent. Capture PR number.
2. `gh pr checkout <pr-number>`.
3. Read into context: plan file, spec file, PR body, issue body. Refuse if no plan file found.
4. Invoke `superpowers:brainstorming` framed around plan revision.
5. Apply user-approved revisions: edit plan file, `git add` + `git commit -m "plan: revise <area>"`, `git push origin HEAD`.
6. If user revises the PR body's `## Plan` section, write the new full body to a temp file and `gh pr edit <pr-number> --body-file <tmpfile>`.
7. Do not mark PR ready or merge.

## Validation & edge cases

- **Spec PR closed mid-flight.** `/plan-issue` reads the PR state at step 3; if the PR closes between then and step 18, `gh pr edit` will fail. Report the failure and stop — the plan commit is on origin but the PR title/body are out of date.
- **Slug mismatch between spec and plan file.** Avoided by extracting the slug from the branch name, not recomputing from the (possibly-edited) issue title.
- **PR body has been hand-edited.** `gh pr view --json body -q .body` returns whatever's currently there. Append `## Plan` to the end as-is. Don't try to "find the discovery report" and merge intelligently.
- **Sweep finds an issue whose spec PR title doesn't start with `Spec:`** (e.g., the user manually retitled it). The head-branch search still matches; we treat it as eligible. The new title is `Spec + Plan: <title>` — but if the original title wasn't `Spec: <title>`, we keep the user's custom title and just add a `[Plan added]` note. **Decision:** simpler — always retitle to `Spec + Plan: <issue-title> (#N)`. If the user wanted a custom title, they'll re-edit after.
- **`gh pr checkout` mid-checkout interruption.** Same risk class as `/spec-issue`. The cleanup step at the end always returns to default branch; if the user `Ctrl-C`s mid-checkout, the working tree is left mid-state, and they'll see the dirty-tree refusal on the next run.
- **Tasks count mismatch.** The `## Plan` section's "Tasks" subsection lists the tasks Claude generated. If counts differ across runs of the same issue (which shouldn't happen because we refuse on existing plan), nothing breaks — the section is informational.

## Out of scope (deferred)

- **Auto-running `/plan-issue` after `/spec-review` exits cleanly.** Manual handoff is more honest — the author decides when the spec is locked.
- **Plan-only PRs (independent of spec).** Out of scope; if needed in future, a `/plan-only` variant would target a non-spec branch.
- **Auto-marking the PR ready-for-review after plan is added.** Always stays a manual user step.
- **Cross-issue plan dependencies.** Each plan is per-issue; if plans need to coordinate, the author handles via spec-level decisions.

## Open follow-ups

- After the first few real generations, check whether the appended `## Plan` PR body section is useful or if reviewers ignore it in favour of the plan file directly. If ignored, switch the body update to a one-line link.
- If `/plan-issues` sweep collides too often with in-flight `/spec-review` work (i.e., users running both), consider a `--dry-run` flag that lists eligible issues without dispatching.
- The PR title change from `Spec: ...` to `Spec + Plan: ...` is one-way — if the user revises the plan via `/plan-review`, the title doesn't need updating. But if they delete the plan file entirely, the title is now misleading. Out of scope for the v1; flag as a known limitation.
